claim: Hand B exhibits the same two-layer terminal morphology (stem + Layer-1 inner + Layer-2 outer) as Hand A, with all five SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 criteria passing under either tokenisation A (MULTI + 'ol') or tokenisation B (MULTI + 'ol' + 'qo'). Sub-test 1a of the H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01 systematic replication of Hand A's morphological characterisation.
evidence_for: Hand A's SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 found 4/5 criteria pass on both tokenisations (MARGINAL — only the C4 ordering ratio failed at 1.12 vs 2.0 threshold). Closed inner inventory (93%), closed outer inventory (86%), productive stems (mean 3.15 distinct inners), 80.6% template coverage all confirmed for Hand A.

Both hands share the manuscript-wide Markov-1-flat character signature (H-BV-MARKOV-VALIDATION-01). If terminal morphology is also shared, the two-layer structural model is a manuscript-level property.
Hand B has more tokens than Hand A (~23,700 vs ~11,000) — better statistical power for detecting closure and productivity criteria.

Locking the test as exact methodology replication of SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 with only the corpus filter changed (B instead of A) maintains pre-registration discipline and methodological lesson 9 (parallel testing on companion corpora).
evidence_against: Hand B's longer mean word length (4.47 vs Hand A 4.24) may shift the closure thresholds — if Hand B has more inner glyph variety, top-10 coverage could fall below the 70% threshold.
Hand B's section composition is more diverse than Hand A's; section-specific vocabulary could affect inner/outer inventory uniformity, potentially changing closure rates.
C4 (ordering) failed on Hand A; the underlying selectional permissiveness is a manuscript-wide property per H-BV-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01. Hand B is likely to also fail C4 for the same reason.
H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 found Hand B has section-concentrated tokens — this might affect the ordering ratio in either direction.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — IDENTICAL METHODOLOGY TO H-BV-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 with corpus filter changed from currier_language=='A' to currier_language=='B'.

  DATA: Hand B tokens from voynich-corpus.json (currier_language=='B').

  TOKENISATIONS (LOCKED, identical to SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01):
    A) MULTI_GLYPHS + 'ol' = ['ckh','cth','cph','ch','sh','ol']
    B) MULTI_GLYPHS + 'ol' + 'qo' = ['ckh','cth','cph','ch','sh','ol','qo']
    Longest-match-first.

  For each tokenisation:
    Restrict to tokens with N >= 3 glyph-units.
    For each w = [g_1, ..., g_N]: STEM = g_1..g_{N-2}, INNER = g_{N-1}, OUTER = g_N.

  FIVE CRITERIA (LOCKED, identical thresholds to SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01):
    C1 INNER CLOSURE: top-10 inners cover >= 70% of inner positions.
    C2 OUTER CLOSURE: top-5 outers cover >= 70% of outer positions.
    C3 PRODUCTIVITY: mean distinct inners per productive stem (>=3 occurrences) >= 2.0 AND >= 40% of productive stems take >= 2 distinct inners.
    C4 ORDERING: P(outer in top-5 | inner in top-10) / P(outer in top-5 | inner not in top-10) >= 2.0 with both conditionals non-trivial.
    C5 COVERAGE: (top-10 inner) x (top-5 outer) template covers >= 60% of N>=3 tokens.

  PER-TOKENISATION VERDICT:
    CONFIRMED if all 5 pass; MARGINAL if 3-4 pass; REFUTED if 0-2 pass.

  OVERALL TWO-TOKENISATION VERDICT (matches SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 framework):
    Both A and B CONFIRMED -> CONFIRMED morphology robust to qo treatment
    Only A CONFIRMED -> CONFIRMED, qo is NOT a ligature
    Only B CONFIRMED -> CONFIRMED, qo IS a ligature
    Both REFUTED -> REFUTED morphology flat
    Mixed -> MARGINAL

  PARALLEL COMPARISON: report side-by-side Hand A (from SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01) vs Hand B (this test) per-criterion and overall verdict, to enable batch-level synthesis.
test_threshold: All 5 pass = CONFIRMED. 3-4 = MARGINAL. <=2 = REFUTED. Overall maps to four named outcomes per SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01.

tests_run.interpretation: Strong replication of Hand A's pattern. Both hands have IDENTICAL 5-criterion outcome (4/5 MARGINAL with C4 ordering as the sole failure). Hand B inner closure 96% (Hand A 93%); outer closure 91% (Hand A 86%); productivity mean 2.79 (Hand A 3.15); coverage 88% (Hand A 81%). Hand B's C4 ratio (1.14) is essentially identical to Hand A's (1.12). The two-layer terminal morphology with closed inventories, productive stems, high template coverage, and weak ordering is a manuscript-wide structural property.

reasoning: Sub-test 1a of the systematic Hand B replication batch. Locks identical methodology to H-BV-SUFFIX-SEQUENCE-01 with only the corpus filter changed (B instead of A). Decision rule and thresholds locked verbatim from the Hand A test for direct comparability. Methodology lesson 9: parallel testing on companion corpora — do not assume extension. The result determines whether Hand A's 4/5 MARGINAL pattern (closed inventories, productive stems, 80%+ coverage, but ordering failure) reproduces in Hand B.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-18 sub-test 1a of H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01 systematic Hand B replication of Hand A morphological characterisation.
